facts.statement: Amgen is identified in SEC submissions as a Nasdaq-listed company with SIC description 'Biological Products, (No Diagnostic Substances)'.
Amgen reported FY2025 revenue of $28.19 billion.
Amgen reported FY2025 net income of $6.717 billion.
Amgen reported FY2025 operating cash flow of $8.471 billion.
Amgen reported FY2025 cash and equivalents of $11.973 billion.
Amgen reported FY2025 long-term debt of $56.549 billion.
Amgen reported FY2025 stockholders' equity of $3.661 billion.
The company says its mission has been to serve patients for more than 45 years.
Amgen describes its innovative and biosimilar medicine portfolios as serving millions of patients globally.
In the 2026 proxy statement, Amgen said 18 of its products achieved record sales in 2025, 14 products exceeded $1 billion in sales, and 13 products delivered double-digit sales growth.
Amgen said IMDELLTRA received full FDA approval for second-line or later treatment of adult patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer.
Amgen said LUMAKRAS, in combination with Vectibix, received FDA approval for third-line treatment of KRAS G12C-mutated metastatic colorectal cancer.
Amgen said UPLIZNA received FDA approvals for immunoglobulin G4-related disease and generalized myasthenia gravis.
Amgen said TEZSPIRE received FDA approval for chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps.

Amgen said it initiated and advanced six global Phase 3 studies for MariTide across four obesity and obesity-related conditions: chronic weight management, cardiovascular outcomes, heart failure, and obstructive sleep apnea.
Amgen said it announced $900 million of additional investment in Ohio and $650 million in Puerto Rico in 2025, following a 2024 announcement of a $1 billion expansion of North Carolina facilities.

Amgen said these U.S. manufacturing expansions are intended to support increased drug production, integrate advanced process development technologies, and improve yield, resilience, and flexibility of its global supply network.
Amgen disclosed that the final U.S. repatriation tax payment of $1.8 billion was made in 2025 under the 2017 Tax Act installment election.
Robert A. Bradway is identified in the proxy materials as Chairman of the Board, Chief Executive Officer, and President.

Amgen's 2026 annual meeting agenda includes election of 12 directors for terms expiring at the 2027 annual meeting, an advisory vote on executive compensation, and ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as independent registered public accountants for fiscal 2026.
The board recommended voting against a stockholder proposal to require an independent board chairman.
Amgen said it engaged in governance-focused outreach with stockholders holding approximately 59% of its outstanding shares since the prior annual meeting.
Amgen disclosed patent and biosimilar litigation involving Prolia/XGEVA, PAVBLU, KYPROLIS, and TAVNEOS, including requests for injunctions and damages in multiple U.S. federal courts.

Amgen disclosed that a Delaware jury returned a verdict against it in the Regeneron antitrust case on antitrust and tortious interference claims, while Amgen stated it had not recorded a liability because it did not consider a loss probable.
